121968.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief supports freedom to conduct business and advocates for a pleasant living and working environment. If expansion is necessary for business activity and employment, we support this.

121969.explanation: Fortunately, in our municipality this responsibility lies with the entrepreneur themselves. An entrepreneur may decide when to open their business.

121970.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief advocates helping our own residents find housing first.

121972.explanation: Our municipality has little agricultural land. Solar panels would especially not be a problem on roofs, both on homes and greenhouses. Much potential remains here.

121973.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief believes this money could be better spent elsewhere in education. Swimming is often an extracurricular activity. Encouraging children to swim can also take place outside of school.

121974.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief supports all forms of construction. We have contributed to making up for lost ground in the construction of social rental housing. Eerlijk Alternatief believes that affordable home ownership should not suffer as a result.

121975.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief believes more housing must be built. This inevitably means building higher. However, we think it must fit into the surroundings and the village character must be preserved. So building along the railway line or on the outskirts of the village is no problem for us.

121976.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief welcomes any form of creating additional living space.

121977.explanation: We have already achieved this in our municipality. Eerlijk Alternatief has also worked hard to make this happen.

121979.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to receive supplies. Furthermore, it makes our center less attractive to visit. If people stay away, it has consequences for our entrepreneurs and for having a lively and pleasant center.

121981.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief believes the residents of this municipality must come first and, where necessary, be able to have their say in major decisions.

121982.explanation: We prefer to leave this to people with expertise. This can be done in cooperation with the neighborhood.

121984.explanation: Fortunately, this is already the case in our municipality. Eerlijk Alternatief has been the champion for this.

121985.explanation: We believe sufficient resources are available through other channels. We do not think the municipality should step in. After all, the municipality is not a bank.

121987.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief believes that refugee housing should not take place in the Randstad. The housing pressure here is already too high. We think municipalities that are shrinking rather than growing should be considered for this purpose.

121989.explanation: We leave it to the residents themselves whether they want to make use of this or not.

121992.explanation: In our opinion, there are recycling centers where this can be disposed of free of charge. If the resident wishes to have it collected, costs may be associated with this in our view.

121997.explanation: This is already standing policy and we gladly continue this.

121998.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief believes people who are struggling financially should be helped. But helping all children in our municipality is financially not feasible.

122001.explanation: There has recently been an expansion. Eerlijk Alternatief has worked hard for this. Especially considering the growing nuisance in, among other places, the Parade and from fatbikes, enforcement officers are needed. They can work alongside the police.

122002.explanation: Especially when it concerns our safety. Eerlijk Alternatief is also in favor of camera surveillance as prevention against illegal dumping of waste in places where (bicycle) theft and nuisance frequently occur.

122009.explanation: As far as we are concerned, the municipality does not specifically have to pay for this, but can encourage and mediate between owners and project developers.

122010.explanation: As far as Eerlijk Alternatief is concerned, this is indeed the basis.

122012.explanation: This has recently been raised to 130% of the social assistance norm, partly on our initiative. So it has been done, but it does not necessarily have to be higher.

122013.explanation: We think this will result in more nuisance from young people.

122014.explanation: We are already well above the national standard in the municipality. It also puts much more pressure on parking spaces for people who do not drive electric vehicles. This is unfair.

122015.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief prefers to keep the situation as it is now.

122019.explanation: For Eerlijk Alternatief, quality is more important than price.

122020.explanation: Eerlijk Alternatief has not yet heard any indications that there is a need for this.

121968.explanation: Any expansion of industrial areas will be considered in conjunction with other priorities such as housing and green/recreation areas.

121969.explanation: This is already permitted in Pijnacker-Nootdorp.

121970.explanation: It is important that the municipality retains the option to give priority to people with a residence permit for social rental housing. But this does not mean they always get priority over other urgent cases. Hence 'neither'.

121972.explanation: Other locations are preferred (such as rooftops, parking lots).

121973.explanation: From the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture, swimming lessons can be paid for low-income families.

121975.explanation: See election program. However, we want to concentrate this on 'logical' locations such as near public transport hubs or certain main roads.

121976.explanation: Disagree. D66 only wants to allow room rental if the owner demonstrably also lives there (host arrangement).

121977.explanation: D66 advocates for lively village centers. This contributes to livability and local entrepreneurship.

121981.explanation: We are obviously positive about a citizen initiative.

121982.explanation: We are positive towards initiatives by residents.

121984.explanation: Our election program states: D66 wants entrepreneurs to have the space to start, grow, and adapt to changes. We simplify rules or even abolish them.

121985.explanation: We support the sustainability loan and want sustainability and the energy transition to remain affordable for everyone.

121986.explanation: This is currently not an issue. If it becomes one, we will make an integral assessment with other priorities such as different types of industrial sites, housing, and green/recreation.

121987.explanation: To comply with the dispersion law, an asylum seekers' center must be established.

121989.explanation: We do not directly advocate a ban, but we are not in favor of burning wood. Our election program states: Fine dust resulting from wood burning will be further reduced through information campaigns.

121992.explanation: The current recycling centers function well. This is not the way to combat illegal dumping. Paid bulky waste collection could be an option.

121997.explanation: In the past council period, we have already invested heavily in child-friendly, sustainable, and future-proof school buildings. This will remain a top priority for D66 in the coming council period.

121998.explanation: Our election program states: we help people with low incomes participate in sports and culture, for example through the Youth Fund for Sport and Culture and the Adult Fund.

122001.explanation: Everyone should feel safe on the streets. This also means having enough enforcement officers (boa's).

122002.explanation: If there is cause, (temporary) camera surveillance can be a good measure. But it is not mandatory.

122009.explanation: There is a great urgency to create more housing. The municipality will consider a contribution only if the transformation would otherwise not take place.

122012.explanation: It is of societal importance that people do not live in poverty.

122013.explanation: D66 believes that young people deserve a fair chance. Together with local entrepreneurs and educational institutions, the municipality looks for a suitable solution for the youth.

122019.explanation: It is always about the combination of quality and price. It is important that the young person is helped properly. In the long term, this yields more (social) benefits than choosing the lowest price now.

122020.explanation: We want the municipality to be a lively, inviting, and safe place, also for our youth. An initiative for a discotheque fits well with that.

121968.explanation: The industrial areas in Pijnacker-Nootdorp are full, while there is demand for more space. We earn our living on these sites, so we need to expand them.

121969.explanation: We want entrepreneurs to be allowed to open every day of the year if they wish, including Sundays and public holidays, 365 days a year.

121970.explanation: We do not give refugees or status holders priority for social housing. We allocate as many social rental homes as possible to local residents.

121972.explanation: We do not want to ban it, but large solar fields can only exist if a local company owns them and there is no nuisance from the solar field.

121973.explanation: School swimming is not a core task of the municipality and is therefore not paid for by the municipality.

121974.explanation: We aim for a healthy mix of owner-occupied and social rental housing. Fixed minimum percentages do not fit with this.

121975.explanation: We want more high-rise buildings, especially along the railway. This way we can build more homes on the same land, for example for starters and seniors.

121976.explanation: Subletting (verkameren) is allowed, provided there are not too many residents per house and no nuisance arises for the neighborhood.

121977.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. Moreover, all our village centers already have their own local center.

121979.explanation: Driving is part of life, also in town centers for shopping or for people with reduced mobility. Cars should still be able to enter the center. However, we encourage cycling and walking.

121981.explanation: The municipal council is elected to make decisions. Referendums do not fit with that.

121982.explanation: If residents can do it better and also do so structurally, VVD allows this (right to challenge).

121984.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves know best when they want to be open or closed. VVD gives them that freedom.

121985.explanation: We support such initiatives with low-threshold permits and cooperative civil servants, but not with direct financial support.

121986.explanation: Business activity and entrepreneurship belong in our municipality, so do distribution centers. Moreover, our industrial areas are full and it is unlikely that a new distribution center will be added soon.

121987.explanation: VVD opposes an asylum seekers’ center in our municipality.

121989.explanation: VVD stands for freedom, which also includes being allowed to light the fireplace occasionally.

121992.explanation: VVD wants to counteract waste dumping. Free bulky waste collection at home is part of that.

121997.explanation: Many school buildings in our municipality are relatively new or recently maintained; extra funds are therefore not necessary.

121998.explanation: Subsidized music lessons are not a core task of the municipality. Parents must pay for their children’s music lessons themselves, just like other hobbies or activities.

122001.explanation: There are many industrial areas and village centers where there is insufficient supervision, especially at night. Additional enforcement officers enable more supervision.

122002.explanation: To combat nuisance, illegal dumping, and street intimidation, we place extra cameras. Especially at industrial areas and shopping centers.

122009.explanation: In Pijnacker-Nootdorp we have no vacant offices suitable for this purpose.

122010.explanation: No benefits without a counter-performance; a fair exchange is required.

122012.explanation: Working must always pay off. A higher income limit for assistance does not help with that.

122013.explanation: We find the current expenditure good; it does not need to be reduced.

122014.explanation: The market naturally follows demand. If there are more electric cars, the charging stations will follow automatically. There is already a concession to place stations when there is demand.

122015.explanation: We only want to reduce speed in truly dangerous situations, not lower the speed throughout the entire built-up area.

122019.explanation: Not only based on price, but also on quality.

122020.explanation: There is no need for that; discos are no longer relevant nowadays.

121968.explanation: There is still space on the business parks themselves for new companies.

121970.explanation: They have become our own residents and at that moment they have no home.

121972.explanation: Solar energy can also contribute to the energy transition.

121973.explanation: Every child needs a swimming diploma and not everyone can afford that.

121974.explanation: We even have higher ambitions, as there is a large shortage of social rental housing in Pijnacker-Nootdorp (in P-N 17% are social rental homes).

121975.explanation: That is appropriate in the centers and along the Randstadrail.

121976.explanation: Subletting can be a way to solve the housing shortage.

121977.explanation: This is how the municipality is structured and we attach great value to shopping centers and other facilities in the village centers.

121979.explanation: We do see opportunities to reduce car traffic in the center.

121981.explanation: We think a citizens' assembly is a good idea.

121982.explanation: We are open to all good ideas and promote community spirit.

121984.explanation: The current rules regarding opening hours are sufficient.

121985.explanation: The current initiatives survive without financial support from the municipality; we do attach great importance to this.

121986.explanation: If it fits with the current business parks, that should be possible.

121987.explanation: Every municipality must take its responsibility.

121989.explanation: It is important that owners of fireplaces adhere to the burning guidelines.

121992.explanation: Nothing is free, because ultimately all residents pay for it.

121998.explanation: Parents with financial means can pay for that themselves.

122001.explanation: The number has recently been greatly expanded.

122002.explanation: If necessary, temporary cameras can be installed, as recently at the Parade.

122009.explanation: If we had those or get them in the future, we think it's a good idea.

122010.explanation: If there are opportunities, it is important that everyone can participate. We do not make it mandatory.

122012.explanation: More and more people are falling into poverty.

122020.explanation: It is not up to the municipality, but up to an entrepreneur to take this initiative.

121968.explanation: Renovating and restructuring existing business parks instead of expanding.

121969.explanation: Trots wants them to have the space and trust to do business without unnecessary rules or obstacles.

121970.explanation: Trots wants residents who live, work, care, and are rooted here to have a greater chance of being able to stay living in their own village again.

121972.explanation: Solar panels on roofs, industrial halls, and parking lots (solar on roofs first). No solar fields that affect open landscapes.

121973.explanation: It is unclear how much it should cost.

121974.explanation: At least 30% mid-rent or social rent. However, in recent years we have seen that social rental homes in Pijnacker-Nootdorp often go to people from outside our municipality, while our own residents sometimes wait for years. This is unacceptable.

121975.explanation: Trots does not want us to lose our village character through building. Pijnacker-Nootdorp is not an urban extension of surrounding cities.

121976.explanation: Creative and innovative housing solutions are needed, such as tiny houses, self-build, and experimental residential neighborhoods.

121977.explanation: Our sports clubs, libraries, cultural associations, and meeting places are essential to that village character. They bring generations together, provide structure to daily life, and make people feel at home in their neighborhood and their village.

121979.explanation: For many residents, the car is not a luxury but a necessary part of daily life.

121981.explanation: Real participation means listening, allowing space, giving feedback, and showing what is done with input. We want an advisory referendum for major decisions.

121984.explanation: The municipality must monitor a healthy balance between livability and vibrancy.

121985.explanation: Trots acknowledges that sustainability is necessary, but we remain grounded. No expensive dreams or imposed obligations, but solutions that work and are affordable.

121986.explanation: We do not want anonymous distribution centers that do not fit the scale and appearance of our villages.

121987.explanation: Trots believes that Pijnacker-Nootdorp should remain a village municipality. Large-scale asylum reception simply does not fit. An asylum seekers' center puts heavy pressure on housing, schools, care, safety, enforcement and social cohesion in our villages.

121989.explanation: Trots wants our residents to be able to live in a clean, healthy and pleasant environment.

121992.explanation: Improve waste separation, but no unrealistic measures.

121997.explanation: We want a school building to be a safe environment where children spend a large part of their youth comfortably. Trots believes buildings should be renewed or expanded in time so children do not end up in outdated or too cramped classrooms.

121998.explanation: Trots believes that art, culture and reading should remain accessible to everyone. We want to support music education.

122001.explanation: Trots believes safety depends on presence. That is why we want police and community officers to be seen more often on the streets, in shopping areas, at sports fields, stations, nature areas and parks. Not only during the day, but especially in the evenings and weekends.

122002.explanation: We want to deploy more modern tools to keep the village safe.

122009.explanation: We want to prevent vacancy by smart reuse of buildings. No subsidies are needed for this.

122010.explanation: We believe support should be based on fairness and reciprocity. People who can work, do volunteer work or contribute in another way should do so.

122012.explanation: By structurally paying attention to poverty reduction, we want to prevent residents from receiving help only when problems have become large.

122013.explanation: We want more visibility of youth work on the street and in neighborhoods.

122014.explanation: Trots wants prevention and therefore opts for parking with realism, where we want more space for cars. We align parking needs with the ever-changing composition of the neighborhood.

122015.explanation: For traffic flow, 50 km/h on main roads is fine and safe. In residential areas, 30 km/h is necessary for livability.

122019.explanation: We should primarily look at whether the help is effective.

122020.explanation: First there must be a good location. Also for other events.

121968.explanation: The CDA stands for an honest and strong economy. It is important that there remains room for new business activity in our municipality.

121969.explanation: Pijnacker-Nootdorp has no Sunday shopping days.

121970.explanation: Every municipality is obliged to accommodate a number of status holders. Even when priority stops, our municipality retains this legal obligation. With the current housing shortage, the priority for status holders must be better balanced against other groups who also have priority for shelter.

121972.explanation: The municipality cannot prohibit this. The CDA prefers solar panels on roofs rather than land. We follow the preferred sequence for generating solar energy: first on roofs and only last on agricultural and nature areas.

121973.explanation: Swimming skills of our children are important. The CDA is open to initiatives for school swimming.

121974.explanation: The Housing Act stipulates that 30% of new construction must be social housing. CDA Pijnacker-Nootdorp wants to comply with the laws. The CDA favors a good mix of affordable owner-occupied and rental homes in various segments.

121975.explanation: As far as the CDA is concerned, high-rise buildings are the exception rather than the rule. Concretely, this means: not too high within existing neighborhoods, but near metro stations and village centers, buildings can be higher.

121976.explanation: Part of society has a need for room rental. For example, students or young people. As long as this does not harm livability, this can be a small-scale solution to the housing shortage.

121977.explanation: Pijnacker, Nootdorp and Delfgauw have a nice village center. In Oude Leede we have the community center.

121979.explanation: No longer allowing cars in Pijnacker center would not be good for retailers. The CDA wants no through truck traffic in Pijnacker center, except for public transport and suppliers.

121981.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a referendum. The municipal council is elected to make difficult trade-offs. It is important to involve citizens in other ways on important topics.

121982.explanation: The CDA supports social initiatives and the right to challenge.

121984.explanation: Opening hours for commercial hospitality businesses, except for terraces, are already free in Pijnacker-Nootdorp. That should remain so.

121985.explanation: The CDA supports energy cooperatives and local residents who take initiatives to make the environment more sustainable. The municipality can support this.

121986.explanation: New companies must add value to the local economy and employment needs of current residents. The establishment of distribution centers must be assessed against this. The CDA wants to counteract the spread of commercial development.

121987.explanation: We do not take a step forward to realize an asylum seekers' center. The CDA wants regional agreements on asylum reception and housing under provincial management.

121989.explanation: A ban on the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves creates a lot of regulatory pressure and is hard to enforce. The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure in society. CDA Pijnacker-Nootdorp is in favor of a burning ban during a burning alert from the RIVM (National Institute for Public Health and the Environment).

121992.explanation: Nothing is free. Waste processing costs money. We pay waste disposal charges for that. The CDA wants to keep these charges as low as possible.

121997.explanation: The municipality has the duty to provide housing (new construction or making a building available) for primary and secondary schools.

121998.explanation: The municipality cannot pay for everything. For children of parents with low income, the municipality offers various provisions. Through the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture, the municipality reimburses fees and material costs for music and cultural lessons for children.

122001.explanation: We have enough community officers. CDA Pijnacker-Nootdorp wants better visible enforcement and more targeted deployment of community officers.

122002.explanation: The CDA wants (fixed or mobile) camera surveillance when it can contribute to increasing safety.

122009.explanation: Due to the higher price per square meter of living space, subsidizing conversion is not necessary. CDA Pijnacker-Nootdorp wants to repurpose long-term vacant buildings and offices within five years.

122010.explanation: The CDA believes people should give something back in the form of socially useful work, such as volunteer work, when they receive unemployment benefits.

122012.explanation: The CDA finds it unacceptable that the municipality you live in determines how well you can make ends meet. Furthermore, increasing benefits and allowances could make working less attractive. The CDA does not want that.

122013.explanation: There is no need to cut back on youth work.

122014.explanation: The CDA considers it important to have sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work. Residents should be able to submit an application to the municipality for this.

122015.explanation: The CDA wants you to be able to safely drive at 50 km/h on main roads within built-up areas. In residential streets and neighborhoods, the standard is 30 km/h.

122019.explanation: Youth care is about much more than just price. For the CDA, the expertise of professionals, average duration, effectiveness, and the manner of (client-oriented) service delivery are at least as important in the selection process.

122020.explanation: The CDA does not see a large nightclub as fitting with the village character of our communities. In the nearby cities of Delft, The Hague, and Rotterdam, there is sufficient provision for those who want to party in a nightclub.

121968.explanation: Business parks that have no future or cause too much nuisance for surrounding residential areas are made circular or transformed into residential neighborhoods + green spaces. When restructuring business parks with a future perspective, more efficient use is made of the limited space.

121969.explanation: The Party for the Animals opposes the 24-hour economy and supports consuming less.

121970.explanation: The municipality meets the housing target for status holders and opposes the bill that removes priority for status holders for social rental housing.

121972.explanation: Agricultural land is not meant for solar parks, but for plant-based food production and nature development and, if livestock farming shrinks substantially, for housing construction.

121973.explanation: The municipality invests in sports, swimming lessons, culture, music lessons, and (independent) nature and sustainability education in schools.

121974.explanation: More affordable housing will be built: at least 40% of rental homes are social rental housing.

121975.explanation: It depends on the height of the apartment blocks. The Party for the Animals wants a maximum of 8 floors, depending on the location.

121976.explanation: That already happens and rules have been established that the PvdD has agreed to.

121977.explanation: The municipality strengthens local initiatives and the formation of resident groups (such as neighborhood/village councils and neighborhood associations). These groups are actively involved in decisions about the neighborhood and can participate in execution and maintenance through the right to challenge.

121979.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports promoting cycling and public transport. The center may become more car-restricted. We adhere to the STOMP principle: first walk, then bike, then public transport, then Mobility as a Service, and finally private car.

121981.explanation: Advisory referenda will be held for important decisions, such as major construction projects or a municipal reorganization. We also enable referenda at the neighborhood level.

121982.explanation: The municipality becomes edible: more food forests and vegetable and pick-your-own gardens will be established, and more plant-based community-supported agriculture (CSA), where residents jointly take ownership and collaborate with the farmer and/or landowner.

121984.explanation: To limit nuisance, it is good to set closing time rules.

121985.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes residents should be able to receive (financial) support to become more sustainable.

121986.explanation: The Party for the Animals opposes the establishment of new large distribution centers, the 'urbanization' of the landscape.

121987.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants a reception center for asylum seekers (azc) in the municipality. The right location can be chosen in consultation with residents.

121989.explanation: The municipality completely prohibits wood burning, both indoors and outdoors. Wood burning causes enormous amounts of fine particulate matter and leads to health problems for many residents. The municipality provides a reporting point for wood burning nuisance and informs residents about the disadvantages of wood burning.

121992.explanation: Picking up or dropping off bulky waste is free up to a certain amount. Pick-up services have costs associated.

121997.explanation: Investments may certainly be made in making buildings more sustainable, but also from a health perspective, such as good ventilation.

121998.explanation: Culture is important and every child must have the opportunity to develop his/her talent.

122001.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants more special enforcement officers (boa's), including domain 2 boa's who can supervise environmental safety, especially in rural areas and greenhouse zones.

122002.explanation: Camera surveillance in public spaces is used very cautiously for privacy reasons. It may only be temporary and under strict conditions, such as a maximum retention period for the camera footage.

122009.explanation: Vacant offices are a great way to tackle the housing shortage.

122010.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits who can contribute to society can do socially meaningful work in return, but not everyone is able to do so and the work must be appropriate. Therefore, no obligation.

122012.explanation: Rising fixed costs and caring for others make life expensive. Unexpected bills, like repairs, are then just too much. The municipality can and should do more to alleviate and prevent poverty.

122013.explanation: Youth workers are deployed. A youth worker who knows the school and students, builds a trusting relationship with them, and regularly visits to talk about topics such as drugs, weapons, (cyber)bullying and (online) sexual abuse and exploitation can prevent much suffering.

122014.explanation: The municipality ensures sufficient charging points with green electricity for electric vehicles.

122015.explanation: In principle, a maximum speed of 30 km/h applies to motorized traffic within built-up areas.

122019.explanation: It is about the price/quality ratio. That is why PN has its own core team with high-quality people and does not need to hire an organization, which is more expensive.

122020.explanation: So far there has been no need for that. If there ever is, it can be discussed.

121968.explanation: There are still sufficient possibilities within the current business parks.

121969.explanation: We stand for an economy that serves society. An economy that does not revolve around more and more, but where human well-being is central and where it is normal to be able to catch your breath. We would like to break the 24/7 economy with Sunday rest.

121970.explanation: Every municipality has a responsibility in hosting asylum seekers, so that there is a national distribution, and we cooperate in this. We also coordinate with regional municipalities. Residents are well and timely informed about changes in size, location, or target group of the reception facilities.

121972.explanation: Within clusters of greenhouse horticulture, space is offered for innovation, such as cold and heat storage, recycling of irrigation water, new lighting concepts, use of solar energy and geothermal energy, etc.

121973.explanation: Every child in Pijnacker-Nootdorp can swim by the end of primary school age.

121974.explanation: We build in all price ranges. This ensures a healthy mix of affordable and more expensive homes that meet the housing needs of our municipality. We aim for a distribution of 30% affordable, 40% mid-range, and 30% expensive. We give housing corporations space to create a good mix.

121975.explanation: If well integrated urban-planning-wise. This is also necessary to build enough affordable housing.

121976.explanation: On a limited scale. Think of 'rooms with care'.

121979.explanation: Slow traffic in Pijnacker-Nootdorp has priority. This makes our living environment livable, safe, and sustainable. We also take into account people with disabilities by creating an accessible environment for everyone.

121981.explanation: We stand for a government that people can rely on when they can no longer manage themselves, that is just and ensures society thrives. A society where people see and help each other, seek connection, and talk about differences. This is what our democratic rule of law is built on.

121982.explanation: In Pijnacker-Nootdorp, from a position of trust, we will actively work on the 'right to challenge.' This is the right of residents to challenge the municipality and indicate that, for example as a neighborhood or village, they want and can maintain a facility themselves.

121984.explanation: We support a society where we take each other into account and look after one another. A place where everyone feels at home. This also involves some regulation.

121985.explanation: We give opportunities to citizen initiatives and support local energy communities. The municipality supports them with manpower and guarantees. This gives energy cooperatives a fair chance.

121986.explanation: We stand for an economy that serves society. An economy that does not revolve around more and more, but where human well-being is central and where it is normal to have time to catch your breath.

121987.explanation: Our municipality also takes responsibility for housing asylum seekers with a residence status and is committed to finding housing for them. We support small-scale shelter.

121989.explanation: Municipalities must vigorously implement the Clean Air Agreement.

121997.explanation: The municipality must fulfill its legal obligations.

121998.explanation: The municipality ensures that everyone entitled knows how to find the Youth Culture Fund.

122001.explanation: The availability and visibility of the police on the streets and online is an important focus for ChristenUnie-SGP. We welcome the deployment of community enforcement officers in public spaces.

122002.explanation: Camera surveillance only makes sense if it contributes to enforcement.

122009.explanation: Where necessary, we transform vacant retail spaces into housing after changing zoning plans.

122010.explanation: Citizens receiving benefits are expected to provide a 'return service.' This can be in the form of volunteer work or a work experience placement.

122012.explanation: Supporter of applying 130% of the minimum wage.

122013.explanation: Unfortunately, funding from the national government does not keep pace with rising costs.

122014.explanation: The number of charging stations will be expanded wherever possible.

122015.explanation: The maximum speed within built-up areas will be 30 kilometers per hour, except on roads with public transport and main connecting roads where 50 kilometers per hour applies.

122019.explanation: Price and quality criteria apply. We collaborate regionally in purchasing care services.

122020.explanation: There are sufficient facilities in the region.

121968.explanation: We see economic development as a top priority. Strong business parks provide employment, innovation, and opportunities for young people and entrepreneurs.

121969.explanation: Our village centers should also thrive: more hospitality venues are welcome, provided good agreements are made about livability and closing times. And as far as PNV is concerned, entrepreneurs retain the freedom to decide whether to open on Sundays.

121970.explanation: PNV is convinced that the way of housing must change. Giving priority is not a solution.

121972.explanation: Our entrepreneurs and greenhouse growers are the engine of the local economy. PNV wants to preserve and strengthen greenhouse horticulture by focusing on innovation and sustainability.

121974.explanation: PNV emphasizes that the municipality must provide affordable housing for starters, young people, the elderly, and low-income residents. We support the idea that social housing is part of balanced neighborhood development. PNV does not mention a fixed percentage.

121975.explanation: PNV conditionally supports high-rise buildings. If housing shortages, accessibility, and location require it, high-rise can be a solution.

121977.explanation: Our village centers should also thrive: more hospitality venues are welcome, provided good agreements are made about livability and closing times. And as far as PNV is concerned, entrepreneurs retain the freedom to decide for themselves whether to open on Sundays.

121979.explanation: Our entrepreneurs and greenhouse growers are the engine of the local economy. Increasing parking fees does not help with that.

121981.explanation: PNV stands for genuine participation. We don’t need a referendum for every small decision, but for large, impactful plans, residents should have the final say. We want you to feel heard, not just during elections, but every day.

121982.explanation: This must be considered on a case-by-case basis.

121984.explanation: PNV stands for support of entrepreneurs and a strong economic climate for business. This means economic freedom and opportunities for entrepreneurs, not strict closing hour rules.

121985.explanation: PNV supports the energy transition, but only if it remains affordable for residents. We believe in municipal support, such as subsidies for insulation and heat pumps.

121987.explanation: A very large shelter location can lead to social pressure on the immediate surroundings. Large concentrations of people in one place may bring challenges regarding safety, facilities, and social cohesion.

121992.explanation: With paid waste streams, there is a risk of illegal dumping. A neglected neighborhood with lots of weeds and litter invites more pollution and decay. We want Pijnacker, Nootdorp, and Delfgauw to remain villages residents are proud of.

121997.explanation: Education: the youth are the future. Good buildings invite learning.

122001.explanation: Safety is the foundation of a pleasant neighborhood.

122002.explanation: Where there are factual problems: structural nuisance, crime, unsafe locations, and where alternative measures (community police officer, youth work, lighting, public space design) prove insufficient, and where there are clear rules about data, storage, privacy and supervision.

122012.explanation: PNV emphasizes that poverty reduction and social inclusion are important for the municipality. We work from a realistic financial approach: PNV pays attention to the balance between municipal expenses and the financial capacity of residents.

122015.explanation: Traffic safety is not determined solely by the speed limit. Road design, roundabouts, sight lines, lighting and bike paths play an equally important role.

121968.explanation: Business parks are important for employment and a strong local economy. Expansion is possible, provided it is carefully integrated, with attention to livability, accessibility and sustainability, and without compromising the village character of Pijnacker-Nootdorp.

121969.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be given the freedom to decide their opening hours. Sunday shopping can contribute to a lively center. Participation should be voluntary, with respect for peace and livability.

121970.explanation: Everyone who urgently needs housing deserves a fair chance. Structural priority for one group increases pressure on social rental housing and creates feelings of inequality. The solution lies in building more affordable homes for all those seeking housing.

121972.explanation: In Pijnacker-Nootdorp, agriculture mainly consists of greenhouse horticulture. Available space is scarce and important for the local economy and landscape. Therefore, solar panels should preferably be placed on roofs of homes and businesses, not on scarce open spaces.

121973.explanation: Swimming is a basic skill in a water-rich country like the Netherlands. School swimming increases safety and ensures that all children, regardless of their parents' financial situation, learn to swim. This prevents inequality and boosts self-confidence.

121974.explanation: There is a serious shortage of affordable housing. Without enough social rental homes, starters, young people and low-income individuals are excluded. A minimum percentage is needed to maintain mixed neighborhoods and fair housing opportunities.

121975.explanation: New homes are urgently needed, but high-rise residential buildings must fit the character of the surroundings. Densification can take place in suitable locations, such as near public transport and existing centers, with respect for livability and village character.

121976.explanation: Room rental can help alleviate the housing shortage, especially for starters and young people. This must be carefully regulated to prevent nuisance and degradation of neighborhood livability.

121977.explanation: A village center is good, but we want more: every neighborhood deserves its own meeting place. A community center strengthens social cohesion, prevents loneliness and makes care and support more accessible and nearby.

121979.explanation: An attractive center requires a good balance between accessibility and livability. Less car traffic can be desirable, but a completely car-free center could be detrimental for entrepreneurs, the elderly and less mobile residents. Tailored solutions are necessary.

121981.explanation: Residents elect the municipal council to make careful decisions on their behalf. Referendums can simplify complex decisions and raise expectations that cannot always be met. Good participation and consultation are more important than referendums.

121982.explanation: If residents want to take responsibility for their living environment themselves, that deserves support. This can strengthen involvement and social cohesion. The municipality must remain responsible for quality, accessibility and continuity.

121984.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be given room to run their businesses properly. Flexible opening hours can contribute to a lively local economy. The municipality must be able to set limits to prevent nuisance for residents.

121985.explanation: If residents produce sustainable energy together, the municipality should support that. Due to the reduction of net metering and rising costs for feeding back energy, neighborhood batteries are becoming increasingly important. This allows locally generated energy to be used locally and ensures fair distribution of benefits.

121986.explanation: Distribution centers take up a lot of space and cause extra freight traffic. In a municipality with limited space, priority should be given to housing, local employment and the preservation of livability and landscape.

121987.explanation: Shelter is a shared responsibility but must be organized on a small scale and balanced across the communities. Large-scale concentrations undermine integration and support. The form and scale are decisive.

121989.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves is part of residents’ freedom. However, it is important to limit nuisance and health risks, for example through education and avoiding burning in unfavorable weather conditions.

121992.explanation: High thresholds for bulky waste disposal lead to more illegal dumping and extra costs for the community. A low-threshold or free collection scheme prevents litter and is ultimately fairer and cheaper for everyone.

121997.explanation: Good school buildings are essential for children's development, health and safety. Investing in schools is investing in the future. Attention must also be paid to a healthy indoor climate and sustainable buildings.

121998.explanation: Music education is valuable for children. At the same time, we must prevent the gradual emergence of more separate subsidies. The most important thing is that families have sufficient income so that such provisions are basically affordable themselves.

122001.explanation: Safety is important, but more enforcement officers is not always the solution. First, it must be clear where the bottlenecks lie. Prevention, good lighting, youth work and social cohesion are just as important as additional public enforcement officers.

122002.explanation: Camera surveillance can help at specific problem locations but should not become a standard measure. Safety primarily requires prevention, good lighting and social cohesion. Camera surveillance should be targeted and proportionate.

122009.explanation: Converting vacant offices into housing can help address housing shortages. In Pijnacker-Nootdorp, this happens hardly at all. If it does occur, it must first be examined whether this is possible without subsidies and whether it fits the local situation.

122010.explanation: Benefits are a subsistence minimum, not a favor. People usually want to contribute themselves, but mandatory work out of distrust often backfires. The municipality should support the path to work without coercion or conditions.

122012.explanation: The current threshold is around 130%. Raising it to 150% helps some households, but every hard income limit causes a poverty trap. The goal should be that people have sufficient income and that schemes become simpler and fairer.

122013.explanation: Youth work prevents problems and offers young people a safe place and guidance. Cutting youth work often leads to higher social costs and fewer opportunities for youth. Investing in youth is investing in the future.

122014.explanation: Sufficient charging points are needed to enable electric driving. This must be done in a balanced way, with attention to parking pressure in neighborhoods and without residents unnecessarily losing parking spaces.

122015.explanation: 30 km/h within built-up areas increases traffic safety, especially for cyclists and children. Implementation must be careful, with clear road design and attention to traffic flow on main roads.

122019.explanation: With youth care, quality must always come first. Selecting only on price can lead to poorer care and bigger problems in the long run. Good youth care helps children in time and prevents more severe and expensive care later.

122020.explanation: Nightlife options for young people are important for a lively municipality. At the same time, a large nightclub should fit the scale of Pijnacker-Nootdorp and not cause nuisance for neighbors. Small-scale, well-integrated facilities are preferred.

statements.title: A large nightclub should be built in Pijnacker-Nootdorp.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Industrial/business parks in Pijnacker-Nootdorp should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: All population centres in the municipality should get their own village/community centre.

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

titleParts.text: in de gemeente moeten een eigen dorpscentrum krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Residents of Pijnacker-Nootdorp must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Pijnacker-Nootdorp moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Of the newly built homes in Pijnacker-Nootdorp at least 30% must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands currently 28% of homes are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Pijnacker-Nootdorp moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The centre of Pijnacker should become car-free.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: In Pijnacker-Nootdorp the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Pijnacker-Nootdorp moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety should be deployed in Pijnacker-Nootdorp.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Pijnacker-Nootdorp.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow Sunday shopping.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: More tall residential apartment blocks should be allowed in Pijnacker-Nootdorp.
